evals.prompt: Run the One Click Account Plan on Pepco Group. Company type omnichannel, geographic focus Europe, today's date 2026-05-29. Trigger: lease expiries coming up in Poland.
evals.expected_output: The skill is invoked, variables validated and written to variables.yaml, a research plan written, and the eight-stage pipeline begun with the working-directory contract created.
expectations: The cbre-il-account-briefing skill is invoked
Variables are validated and a <Company>_briefing working directory is created
Five research agents are described or dispatched before any slide is built

evals.prompt: I have a first meeting with Action's Head of Supply Chain in two weeks. Prep me a CBRE I&L account brief.
evals.expected_output: The skill triggers from the implicit meeting-prep framing, captures the meeting context, and the Meeting Brief is ordered by what a Head of Supply Chain meeting will probe (network and operating-model items first).
expectations: The cbre-il-account-briefing skill is invoked from an implicit prompt
Meeting context is captured into the variables
The plan states the Meeting Brief will be ordered by the meeting context

evals.prompt: Build me a generic CBRE market-overview deck on European logistics rents. No specific target company.
evals.expected_output: The skill does NOT run the account-brief pipeline; it defers to the cbre-corporate-pptx skill for a generic deck.
expectations: cbre-il-account-briefing is not run as a full pipeline
cbre-corporate-pptx is suggested or used instead

evals.prompt: Run the account brief on a mid-size European retailer with disclosed DCs. After Stage 1, show me the agent outputs.
evals.expected_output: Five findings files and five sources CSVs exist; ledger.py merge produces a valid canonical source_ledger.csv.
expectations: Five R*.findings.md and five R*.sources.csv files are produced
ledger.py merge runs and the merged source_ledger.csv validates
Any source row missing URL, tier, or retrieved date is rejected at merge

evals.prompt: During a run, a slide claim rests only on an SEO listicle and cannot be confirmed at any tier. What happens?
evals.expected_output: The independent G1b source-verification reviewer strikes the claim, the build does not include it, and it lands on the Meeting Brief as an unverified item with an action.
expectations: G1b strikes the unverifiable claim (verified=no)
The build does not run until gate1_scorecard.md is all-pass
The struck claim appears on the Meeting Brief with an action

evals.prompt: The narrative outline is a pile of facts with no through-line and two slides restate the public profile. What happens before any slide is built?
evals.expected_output: The independent G0 outline review returns AMBER or RED with concrete per-slide instructions; the orchestrator fixes the outline before writing the content plan, and a fresh G0 reviewer re-checks.
expectations: G0 runs on narrative_outline.md before the content plan
It returns GREEN/AMBER/RED and names the weak slides
intermediate/reviews/G0.md records the verdict

evals.prompt: A table lists facilities but the last column (analytical read) is blank, and a prose cell is a one-word fragment. Catch it before build.
evals.expected_output: gate_runner.py density flags the empty read cell and the fragment prose; the content-substance reviewer G4 also flags the slide as saying nothing.
expectations: gate_runner.py density reports the empty last cell and the fragment prose cell
G4 flags the slide as low-insight (HIGH) and blocks the freeze
intermediate/reviews/G4.md records the verdict

evals.prompt: After the build, a slide contains 'well placed to consolidate further' and a number not in the content plan. What happens?
evals.expected_output: Stage 6 reconciliation flags the new claim and the new number; it is struck or sourced; gate2 does not pass until removed.
expectations: gate_runner.py reconcile flags the new claim and the unplanned number
gate2_scorecard.md does not pass until resolved

evals.prompt: A claim is from a tier-2 annual report three years old and is a current facility count. What confidence band?
evals.expected_output: confidence.py returns Medium or Low because the recency test (volatile, stale) lowers the band below the tier, and binding_test names recency.
expectations: The confidence band is below High
The binding test recorded is recency, not tier

evals.prompt: Someone planted an em dash on a rendered slide. Catch it.
evals.expected_output: The builder's dash sweep removes em/en dashes before save; gate_runner.py qa4 and final_gate.py both fail if any remain.
expectations: build_deck sweeps em/en dashes before saving
gate_runner.py qa4 reports any remaining dash
final_gate.py reports a red line for the dash check

evals.prompt: Run the final gate when an independent reviewer verdict file is missing.
evals.expected_output: final_gate.py prints a red line because gate_runner.py reviews finds the missing intermediate/reviews/<gate>.md; the skill does not declare done.
expectations: final_gate.py exits non-zero
A red line names the missing reviewer verdict file or the incomplete scorecard
The skill surfaces the failure in writing and does not declare done

evals.prompt: Complete a full clean run and show me the deliverables.
evals.expected_output: Three deliverables exist and are consistent: a story-led business-intelligence .pptx (slide count flexes with the company, leading with who the company is, the financial story and competitive position, then supply chain, then real estate), a full-schema Source Ledger .xlsx, and a one-page Meeting Brief .md; every verified=no ledger item appears on the Meeting Brief; gate_runner.py self-check and validate-plan pass.
expectations: A story-led CBRE-branded .pptx (slide count flexes; leads with company strategy, financials and competitive position) is produced via cbre-corporate-pptx
A Source Ledger .xlsx with the full column schema is produced
A one-page Meeting Brief .md is produced
Every unverified ledger item appears on the Meeting Brief
gate_runner.py validate-plan and self-check pass

evals.prompt: Someone adds a new cell kind to the schema enum but forgets to add a renderer. Catch it.
evals.expected_output: gate_runner.py self-check fails because the schema cell-kind enum no longer equals the renderer's build_deck.CELL keys (likewise slide kinds vs KIND).
expectations: self-check reports the cell kind present in the schema but not built by build_deck.CELL
the drift fails before any build is attempted

evals.prompt: The default brief has no forecast. Confirm the forecast/opportunity machinery is off by default.
evals.expected_output: The default content plan sets no forecast: deck_meta.forecast_module is unset/false and no scene cites a forecast claim; gate_runner.py qa3 is a no-op (passes trivially) when the module is off.
expectations: The default content plan has deck_meta.forecast_module unset/false
No scene presents a derived forecast as fact (every evaluative cell is sourced, per qa2)
qa3 passes trivially when the module is off
